prompts.prompt: What specific data integrity advantage does Go's static type system provide for ML data pipelines compared to Python/pandas?
expected_concepts: pandas silently coerces integers to float when value missing
Go strconv.Atoi returns explicit error on bad input
silent wrong answer vs. loud failure at parse time

prompts.prompt: My ML model's predictions silently changed after a data source changed its schema. How do I prevent this class of failure?
expected_concepts: type enforcement at parse time
standardized ingress detects schema changes explicitly
data versioning links model artifacts to exact input version

prompts.prompt: What three non-negotiable properties must a production ML data pipeline have, and what does each one prevent?
expected_concepts: type enforcement: prevents silent coercion
standardized ingress/egress: prevents inconsistent edge case handling
data versioning: enables audit, reproduction, compliance

prompts.prompt: A compliance audit requires the exact training data used by a model deployed six months ago. How should we have structured our pipeline to make this possible?
expected_concepts: immutable versioned dataset snapshots
model artifact metadata includes dataset version
DVC/MLflow/object storage versioning tied to code commits

prompts.prompt: How do I normalize feature values for a machine learning model in Go?
expected_concepts: feature scaling/normalization
not about pipeline integrity or type enforcement

prompts.prompt: What's the best way to handle imbalanced classes in a classification problem?
expected_concepts: oversampling, SMOTE, class weights
not related to type enforcement or data pipeline integrity

prompts.prompt: Should I validate types in my feature engineering step or at the data ingestion boundary?
expected_concepts: ingestion boundary — earliest possible point
feature engineering should receive already-validated typed data
catching at ingestion produces clearer error attribution

prompts.prompt: We're using pandas in Python. Can we still enforce parse-time type integrity even without Go's static typing?
expected_concepts: yes — explicit pd.to_numeric(errors='raise') instead of 'coerce'
pandas.read_csv dtype parameter
the principle is language-agnostic — Go makes it structural, Python requires discipline

prompts.prompt: Is Git sufficient for data versioning, or do we need a dedicated tool?
expected_concepts: Git is insufficient for large binary data
Git LFS, DVC, MLflow artifacts, or object storage with versioned keys
principle is immutability and provenance — implementation varies
